governance.governance_hash_note: Value computed at runtime by governance_hash.py (canonical v1.0). Not stored here statically; authoritative values reside in AIAP.md, agent_card.json, and quality_baseline.json after TRI-SYNC write.

mcp_server.note: MCP Server support is prepared but not yet implemented. This manifest serves as schema definition for future activation.

tools.name: snapshot_audit
tools.description: Audit version snapshot integrity — verifies completeness against hard-coded SNAPSHOT_MANIFEST, validates SHA-256 hashes of snapshot files against source originals, checks meta.json canonical fields (including created_at truthfulness validation: rejects 00:00:00 placeholder, out-of-range year, non-ISO strings). Provides --emit-created-at subcommand to emit Python-computed UTC ISO 8601 timestamp to stdout for meta.json created_at field (emit path is output-only, does not write files). Audit main path: READ-ONLY, never writes or modifies any file.

aiap_dir.description: Path to the source AIAP directory

exit_codes.1: FAIL — one or more checks failed

tools.name: governance_hash
tools.description: Deterministic governance_hash computation (canonical v1.0) and ATOMIC TRI-SYNC write. SOLE AUTHORITY for governance_hash values — LLM/ReviewFinalize MUST NOT compute or fill hash values directly. Algorithm: enumerate *.aisop.json sorted ASCII, JCS canonicalize each (json.dumps sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=False), UTF-8 encode, join with 0x1E Record Separator, sha256.hexdigest(). Writes to AIAP.md, AIAP_cn.md (if exists), agent_card.json, quality_baseline.json (top-level governance_hash only — attestation_chain advancement is delegated to attestation_advance.py SOLE AUTHORITY, since the on-disk attestation_chain is a dict the list-only update branch here silently no-ops on). Post-write read-back verification for byte-identical assertion.

program_dir.description: Absolute path to the AIAP program directory containing *.aisop.json files

exit_codes.0: SUCCESS — hash computed and TRI-SYNC verified
exit_codes.1: FAILURE — computation error or read-back mismatch

tools.consumed_by: main.aisop.json ReviewFinalize.step1, review.aisop.json Finalize.step3 (SOLE AUTHORITY for governance_hash)

tools.name: resolve_target
tools.description: Deterministic evolution-target resolution + sovereignty guard (target != runner operand integrity). Replaces the LLM-inferred AUTHORITATIVE TARGET RESOLUTION prose rule (PipelineStart step1(b), B1 v2.30.0) that the sub-agent repeatedly ignored (cache/105). READ-ONLY of all inputs except the _index.json target fields it writes. Reads _index.user_message (authoritative operand source) + _index.target (runner/executor) + workspace_dir; deterministically extracts the explicit target (absolute-path regex first, then '{name}_aiap' name scan); resolves to the actual AISOP directory; runs SOVEREIGNTY GUARD (exit 1 OPERAND_AMBIGUITY when the user named a different explicit target yet it collapses onto the runner; ALLOW when the user explicitly named the runner itself = legitimate engine-style self-evolution); on success atomically writes authoritative _index.target_aiap_dir + pipeline_context.target_aiap_dir + creator_directory. Pure stdlib (argparse/json/os/re/sys); never touches *.aisop.json / AIAP.md / agent_card.json / quality_baseline.json / governance files.

cache_dir.description: Absolute path to the execution cache directory containing _index.json

dry_run.description: --dry-run: resolve + run sovereignty guard and print result, but do NOT write _index.json (read-only verification mode)

exit_codes.0: SUCCESS — target resolved + sovereignty guard passed; _index written unless --dry-run

tools.consumed_by: main.aisop.json PipelineStart.step1(b) Evolve branch (SOLE AUTHORITY for evolution target resolution; HARD_FAIL HALT on TOOL-MISSING or exit 1)

tools.name: attestation_advance
tools.description: Deterministic SOLE-AUTHORITY advancement of quality_baseline.json attestation_chain (dict-aware) + final_adjusted_reconciliation to the current version + current governance_hash. Root-fixes ATTESTATION_CHAIN_STALE (band-out E1 SG-10): the on-disk attestation_chain is a version-keyed DICT, which governance_hash.py's isinstance(chain,list) branch silently no-ops on, freezing the chain several versions behind. SIBLING tool to governance_hash.py — CONSUMES the canonical top-level governance_hash, NEVER recomputes it. Mirrors governance_hash.py pattern: read top-level version+governance_hash -> DICT-AWARE APPEND-ONLY INSERT 'v{current}' entry {version, from=immediately-prior semver-max key no-skip, governance_hash=current, prev pointer} leaving existing entries untouched -> advance top-level attestation_chain_length -> sync evolution_history.{current} final_adjusted/deferred_reconciled note to cite current version+hash (no stale 'synced; <old hash>') -> ATOMIC .tmp+os.replace -> read-back verify. LLM/ReviewFinalize MUST NOT fill these provenance fields directly. --verify-only emits ATTESTATION_CHAIN_STALE (read-only detector).

program_dir.description: Absolute path to the AIAP program directory containing quality_baseline.json

verify_only.description: --verify-only: READ-ONLY ATTESTATION_CHAIN_STALE detector (no writes), reused by ValidateStep.step3 MF29 QUAD-SYNC

current_governance_hash.description: 64-char SHA-256 hex digest (consumed from governance_hash.py, not recomputed)

exit_codes.0: SUCCESS — chain advanced + read-back verified (advance mode) | all-pass (verify-only)
exit_codes.1: FAILURE / ATTESTATION_CHAIN_STALE — read-back mismatch, missing file, or stale chain

tools.consumed_by: review.aisop.json Finalize.step3 (after governance_hash TRI-SYNC AND P23 deferred-score reconciliation; SOLE AUTHORITY for attestation_chain advancement; HARD_FAIL on TOOL-MISSING or exit 1); ValidateStep.step3 MF29 QUAD-SYNC (--verify-only ATTESTATION_CHAIN_STALE detector)

notes: Pattern G preparation - directory structure and manifest created in v2.23.0
Tool implementations will be added in future versions based on ecosystem adoption
MCP integration will be activated when MCP Server support is fully specified
This manifest follows I13 compliance requirements for future tool security
